slides.title: Historical Figures and Legacy
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 8 English, Unit 7: Historical Figures and Legacy. Welcome to Unit 7 of your Year 8 English course. In this unit, you will explore the theme of Historical Figures and Legacy - a topic that invites you to think about how individuals shape history and how their actions echo through generations. You will read a powerful story set in 1948 East Africa, learn essential grammar structures for narrating past events, and build a rich vocabulary for discussing argument, leadership, emotion, and change. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Use the Past Simple and Past Continuous tenses correctly to narrate events and describe actions in progress in the past
Form and use the Past Perfect tense to show which action happened first when telling a story
Convert direct speech into Reported Speech, adjusting pronouns, verb tenses, and word order correctly
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to use the past simple and past continuous tenses correctly to narrate events and describe actions in progress in the past. you will also form and use the past perfect tense to show which action happened first when telling a story. you will also convert direct speech into reported speech, adjusting pronouns, verb tenses, and word order correctly. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: 1. benign, 2. reprieve, 3. defect, 4. disposition, 5. systematic, 6. cache. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
bullets: Historical Figures and Legacy: source text 1
Historical Figures and Legacy: source text 2
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Historical Figures and Legacy: source text 1 and Historical Figures and Legacy: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: How to Form Them
bullets: Past Simple - Regular Verbs: Add -ed to the base form of the verb. play -> played
slides.narration: The first language focus is How to Form Them. Past Simple - Regular Verbs: Add -ed to the base form of the verb. play -> played Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Story Retelling. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
